attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; excels in select group sprints from reduced bunches. Strongest in final 2km positioning battles on technical finishes rather than distance attacks.
team_tactics: Versatile role player: sacrifices for teammates on cobbles, opens sprints when leadout unavailable, but thrives when given protected status with positioning freedom in finales.
terrain_preference: Cobbled classics and technical finales suit best; shows surprising uphill sprint capability. Exposed in pure flat sprints requiring full leadout trains.
vulnerability: Struggles when forced to mark moves within breakaways, exhausting himself before finale. Underperforms when deployed as emergency leadout man rather than protected finisher.
key_patterns: Exceptional resilience: recovers from seemingly losing positions to finish strong (Paris-Roubaix 2022, Tour 2018)
Superior positioning instinct in reduced sprints within final 2km (Omloop 2020, Paris-Nice 2022)
Often miscast in team roles: sacrifices for others or opens sprints when better suited as finisher
Performs best with relaxed approach and protected status rather than heavy workload expectations
Stronger on technical/uphill finales than pure flat sprints requiring traditional leadout
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 showed exceptional resilience at Paris-Roubaix and climbing strength at Paris-Nice, suggesting evolution beyond pure classics sprinter role
